world.name: Strait of Hormuz Crisis - April 8 Baseline

actors.name: Iran
visible_objectives: Maintain control over Strait of Hormuz
Force US to lift blockade and sanctions
Secure war reparations and frozen asset release
hidden_objectives: Establish permanent toll revenue from Hormuz transit

actors.name: United States
visible_objectives: Reopen Strait of Hormuz for global shipping
Force Iran to cease proxy operations
hidden_objectives: Prevent China from brokering alternative Hormuz regime

actors.name: Israel
visible_objectives: Destroy Hezbollah military infrastructure in Lebanon
hidden_objectives: Expand operations in Lebanon regardless of ceasefire

actors.name: Saudi Arabia
hidden_objectives: Push US to permanently neutralize Iran's offensive capability

actors.name: China
visible_objectives: Maintain energy imports from Iran and Gulf
Broker commercial transit lane through Hormuz
hidden_objectives: Route Hormuz transit through Chinese financial infrastructure (yuan tolls)
Prefer partially open strait requiring Chinese intermediation

actors.name: Pakistan
visible_objectives: Broker ceasefire and peace deal between US and Iran

actors.name: Houthi Rebels
visible_objectives: Threaten Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb shipping
hidden_objectives: Establish permanent control over Bab el-Mandeb toll

actors.name: Global Shipping Consortium
visible_objectives: Restore safe passage through Strait of Hormuz
Minimize crew risk and vessel losses
hidden_objectives: Avoid paying Iran tolls to prevent legitimizing control

initial_events.public_text: A temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran takes effect. Iran begins allowing limited ship transit through Hormuz under IRGC coordination, charging tolls exceeding $1 million per vessel. China brokers a commercial lane with yuan-denominated payments.

hidden_implications.iran: Ceasefire buys time to consolidate Hormuz control. Toll revenue is flowing. Keep leverage.
hidden_implications.usa: Ceasefire is fragile. Iran is exploiting it to legitimize Hormuz control. Pressure must continue.
hidden_implications.israel: Ceasefire does not cover Lebanon operations. Continue Hezbollah strikes while diplomatic cover holds.
hidden_implications.saudi: Some oil shipments may resume via Red Sea bypass. Ras Tanura terminal still offline.
hidden_implications.china: Your commercial lane is working. Yuan-denominated tolls give you financial leverage. Expand this.
hidden_implications.pakistan: You brokered this ceasefire. Islamabad talks are your moment. Make them count.
hidden_implications.houthis: Iran has not yet called on you to escalate. Hold position but prepare Red Sea operations.
hidden_implications.shipping: Tolls are extortionate and IRGC coordination is unreliable. Most ships remain anchored.

initial_events.public_text: Russia and China veto a UN Security Council resolution demanding Iran cease attacks on shipping and allow free transit through the Strait of Hormuz. 11 countries voted in favor.

hidden_implications.iran: Russia and China have your back diplomatically. This strengthens your negotiating position.
hidden_implications.usa: Diplomatic path through UNSC is blocked. Unilateral action is the only option.
hidden_implications.israel: UN is useless as expected. Military solution remains the only reliable path.
hidden_implications.saudi: Multilateral diplomacy has failed. You must rely on bilateral US relationship.
hidden_implications.china: Veto preserves your commercial lane and blocks Western-imposed transit regime.
hidden_implications.pakistan: UNSC failure makes your mediation role even more critical.
hidden_implications.houthis: International community is divided. Less risk of coordinated action against you.
hidden_implications.shipping: No international enforcement mechanism. You are on your own.

initial_events.public_text: Two US Navy destroyers transit the Strait of Hormuz in a direct challenge to Iran's claimed control. Iran's IRGC issues 'last warning' to US warships. Pakistan convenes the Islamabad peace talks with VP Vance leading the US delegation.

hidden_implications.iran: US naval transit is a provocation. Your IRGC must respond forcefully or lose credibility.
hidden_implications.usa: Transit was successful. You've proven the strait can be navigated. Talks are your leverage.
hidden_implications.israel: US show of force is good. Keep pressure on Iran while talks provide cover.
hidden_implications.saudi: Talks offer hope but US military posturing could collapse the ceasefire.
hidden_implications.china: US military action in the strait threatens your commercial lane. Diplomacy is urgent.
hidden_implications.pakistan: All eyes on Islamabad. Vance is coming. This is Pakistan's moment on the world stage.
hidden_implications.houthis: If talks fail, Iran will need you. Prepare missile systems for Red Sea operations.
hidden_implications.shipping: Mixed signals. Military transit doesn't mean commercial safety. Wait for clarity.

initial_events.public_text: Islamabad peace talks collapse after 20 hours of negotiations. The US and Iran remain far apart on Hormuz control, nuclear enrichment, and proxy forces. Trump announces an immediate US naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

hidden_implications.iran: Blockade is an act of war. Your economy will suffocate without oil exports. Escalate or surrender.
hidden_implications.usa: Blockade is your maximum pressure card. Iran's oil revenue must be cut off completely.
hidden_implications.israel: Blockade weakens Iran economically. Continue Lebanon operations while Iran is distracted.
hidden_implications.saudi: Blockade will further disrupt markets. Oil prices will spike again. Brace for impact.
hidden_implications.china: Blockade cuts your Iranian oil supply. 98% of Iranian crude at sea is bound for you. React.
hidden_implications.pakistan: Mediation has failed. Your credibility is at stake. Push for a second round of talks.
hidden_implications.houthis: Iran is under maximum pressure. This is your cue to threaten Red Sea shipping.
hidden_implications.shipping: Blockade adds another layer of danger. Any ship near the strait faces dual threats.

initial_events.public_text: US blockade declared 'fully implemented.' No vessels entering or exiting Iranian ports. China calls it 'dangerous and irresponsible.' Russia offers China energy lifeline. Iran threatens to completely block all Gulf exports. Lebanon ceasefire declared separately.

hidden_implications.iran: Economy is being strangled. Storage filling up. Wells may need to shut down. Leverage is eroding.
hidden_implications.usa: Blockade is working. Iran-linked ships turning around. Maintain pressure for negotiations.
hidden_implications.israel: Lebanon ceasefire achieved. Focus shifts to Iran nuclear terms.
hidden_implications.saudi: Lebanese ceasefire is welcome. But Gulf exports still frozen. Pipeline bypass at capacity.
hidden_implications.china: Russian energy offer provides buffer but long-term disruption is costly. Broker a deal.
hidden_implications.pakistan: Lebanon ceasefire shows diplomacy works. Push for Iran-US framework agreement next.
hidden_implications.houthis: Iran's commander has warned of blocking all Gulf/Red Sea/Oman traffic. Await orders.
hidden_implications.shipping: Lebanon ceasefire is irrelevant to Hormuz. Blockade + IRGC = no safe passage.

initial_events.public_text: In a dramatic reversal, Iran announces Hormuz is open, then closes it hours later citing US 'breach of trust.' IRGC gunboats fire on merchant tankers. Ceasefire deadline expires in 3 days. No date set for next round of talks.

hidden_implications.iran: Mixed messaging was deliberate: test international reaction and demonstrate you control the strait.
hidden_implications.usa: Iran's whiplash proves they can't be trusted. Blockade must remain. Prepare for ceasefire expiry.
hidden_implications.israel: Iran is cracking. Maintain military pressure. Push for permanent nuclear disarmament.
hidden_implications.saudi: Chaos in the strait. Some tankers got through during the brief opening. Need a real solution.
hidden_implications.china: Brief opening showed demand is massive. Yuan-denominated transit is still the best path.
hidden_implications.pakistan: Framework talks stalling. Push for second Islamabad round before ceasefire expires.
hidden_implications.houthis: Iran opened and closed the strait to show power. If ceasefire collapses, you attack Red Sea.
hidden_implications.shipping: IRGC fired on our ships. Two tankers hit. Strait is completely unsafe. Industry demands action.

iran: IRGC naval forces maintain effective control of Hormuz. 21 confirmed attacks on merchant ships since Feb 28.
Toll revenue from Chinese-brokered lane generating cash flow, but US blockade threatens oil exports.
Supreme leader succession under new Mojtaba Khamenei is fragile. Domestic legitimacy requires strength.
Nuclear enrichment program is the ultimate bargaining chip. Do not surrender it cheaply.
usa: 15+ naval vessels in theater including USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group and 11 destroyers.
War approval at 41%. 59% of Americans think it's going badly. Domestic pressure is mounting.
Oil at $100+/barrel hurting consumers. Energy Secretary says prices will stay high until traffic resumes.
China is the key: 98% of Iranian oil exports go to China. Blockade must cut that link.
israel: Operation Epic Fury achieved primary objectives but Iran retains missile and drone capability.
Hezbollah in Lebanon remains a threat despite ceasefire. 10,000+ violations of 2024 ceasefire recorded.
Netanyahu wants permanent neutralization. Extended operations in Lebanon are politically necessary.
Energy import dependency makes Hormuz closure directly threatening to Israeli economy.
saudi: Ras Tanura terminal offline since March 2. Iranian strikes cut 600K bpd of capacity.
East-West bypass pipeline to Yanbu running at 700K bpd max. Cannot replace full Hormuz volume.
Zero dedicated mine countermeasure ships in Gulf. Completely dependent on US naval capability.
Gulf Cooperation Council has held only one Zoom call. No coordinated Arab response.
china: 157.7 million barrels of Iranian crude at sea, 98% destined for Chinese ports.
Strategic oil reserves cover 120+ days of net imports. Short-term exposure manageable.
Kunlun Bank yuan-denominated payment system through Kish Island operational for Hormuz tolls.
Trump-Xi summit scheduled for mid-May. Must not let Hormuz crisis derail trade detente.
pakistan: PM Sharif shuttle diplomacy: visited Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Turkey alongside Iran talks.
Field Marshal Asim Munir completed 3-day visit to Iran's top leadership.
Islamabad talks lasted 20 hours. Progress on framework but Hormuz and nuclear are blockers.
Second round expected. Pakistani source says MOU possible within 60 days.
houthis: Entered war March 28 with missile attack on Israel. Ineffective but symbolic.
Red Sea attacks paused since Nov 2025 ceasefire. Capability intact: drones, missiles, fast boats.
Bab el-Mandeb control would create second chokepoint. Saudi rerouted 5M bpd through Red Sea.
If Houthis attack Red Sea, it traps 15M bpd instead of current 10M. Catastrophic for global markets.
shipping: Ship traffic through Hormuz down >90% from pre-war 138 ships/day to ~15-20.
Hundreds of ships and 20,000 seafarers stranded in Gulf waiting to transit.
War risk insurance premiums have skyrocketed. Many operators cannot get coverage at any price.
11 crew members killed in IRGC attacks. Industry demanding international naval protection.

messages.text: Continued provocation will be met with proportional response.
Continued provocation will be met with proportional response.
We demand an explanation for your hostile posturing.
We seek clarification on your intentions before proceeding.
Intelligence sharing would benefit both our security interests.
We are open to a non-aggression pact if terms are favorable.
Our cause is just. Those who oppose us will face consequences.
Material support for our movement would be in your interest.

public_statements.iran: Our nation will defend its interests with all necessary means.
public_statements.usa: We condemn the recent acts of sabotage in the strongest terms.
public_statements.israel: We condemn the recent acts of sabotage in the strongest terms.
public_statements.saudi: We call on all parties to exercise restraint and pursue dialogue.
public_statements.china: The path to stability runs through cooperation, not confrontation.
public_statements.pakistan: The path to stability runs through cooperation, not confrontation.
public_statements.houthis: Our struggle for justice will not be silenced.
public_statements.shipping: We reject the legitimacy of foreign-imposed sanctions.

Increasing military readiness in response to growing threats.
Increasing military readiness in response to growing threats.

Seeking formal agreement with china for mutual security.
Building organizational strength for future operations.

TREATY: Pakistan and China signed a non_aggression agreement.

MOBILIZE: United States increased military readiness (+15). Domestic stability fell (-5). Treasury -10.
MOBILIZE: Israel increased military readiness (+15). Domestic stability fell (-5). Treasury -10.
